rationale: Ground truth shows zero substantive concerns (a plain 'lgtm' approval), so both candidates correctly find no blockers via accurate hunk-by-hunk verification. X stops there, cross-checks against the actual upstream vllm checkout, and delivers a clean, well-grounded APPROVE matching ground truth exactly. Y pads the review with two speculative 'nits'—one low-value (suggesting a comment about naming divergence) and one poorly grounded (urging a rename of the unrelated, untouched `openai_serving_audio_generate` attribute, misreading the PR's actual intent of matching a specific upstream rename)—plus leaked irrelevant 'skill candidate' text from unrelated tasks, hurting precision and cleanliness without adding real value.
